# azure-functions-scaffold

> Scaffolding CLI for production-ready Azure Functions Python v2 projects.

## Package Info

- PyPI: `pip install azure-functions-scaffold`
- Version: 0.3.2
- Python: >=3.10, <3.15
- License: MIT
- Docs: https://yeongseon.github.io/azure-functions-scaffold/
- Repository: https://github.com/yeongseon/azure-functions-scaffold

## What It Does

CLI tool that generates production-ready Azure Functions Python v2 project scaffolds in one command.
Handles boilerplate setup: `host.json`, `function_app.py`, directory structure, tooling config, and
tests. Lets developers focus on business logic from the start.

## CLI Commands

```
azure-functions-scaffold [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
afs [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]                          # Short alias
```

### Main Commands

- `new [PROJECT_NAME]` ‚Äî Create a new Azure Functions project
  - Options: `--template`, `--preset`, `--python-version`, `--github-actions`, `--git`,
    `--with-openapi`, `--with-validation`, `--with-doctor`, `--interactive`, `--dry-run`, `--overwrite`
  - Default template: `http`
  - Default preset: `standard`
  - Default python version: `3.10`
  - Omit PROJECT_NAME to enter interactive mode

- `add TRIGGER FUNCTION_NAME` ‚Äî Add a new function to existing project
  - Arguments: TRIGGER (http, timer, queue, blob, servicebus), FUNCTION_NAME
  - Options: `--project-root`, `--dry-run`
  - Default project root: `.`

- `templates` ‚Äî List available templates
- `presets` ‚Äî List available presets

### Options

- `--version` ‚Äî Show installed version
- `-h`, `--help` ‚Äî Show help

## Templates

| Template | Command | Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| http | `azure-functions-scaffold new my-api` | REST APIs, webhooks |
| timer | `azure-functions-scaffold new my-job --template timer` | Scheduled tasks, cron |
| queue | `azure-functions-scaffold new my-worker --template queue` | Message processing (Azurite) |
| blob | `azure-functions-scaffold new my-blob --template blob` | File processing (Azurite) |
| servicebus | `azure-functions-scaffold new my-bus --template servicebus` | Enterprise messaging |

## Presets

Presets combine tooling levels and best practices:

- `minimal` ‚Äî Bare essentials (host.json, function_app.py)
- `standard` ‚Äî Standard tools (ruff, pytest, mypy)
- `strict` ‚Äî Production-ready (ruff, mypy, pytest, azure-functions-doctor)

## Quick Start

```bash
# Step 1: Install
pip install azure-functions-scaffold

# Step 2: Create project
azure-functions-scaffold new my-api

# Step 3: Install dependencies
cd my-api
pip install -e .

# Step 4: Run locally
func start

# Open http://localhost:7071/api/hello in browser
```

## Optional Features

Enable by adding flags to `new` command:

- `--with-openapi` ‚Äî Swagger UI + OpenAPI spec endpoints
- `--with-validation` ‚Äî Pydantic request/response validation
- `--with-doctor` ‚Äî azure-functions-doctor health checks
- `--github-actions` ‚Äî Basic GitHub Actions CI workflow
- `--git` ‚Äî Initialize git repository in project

## Generated Project Layout

```
my-api/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ function_app.py          # v2 entrypoint
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ host.json                # Runtime config
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ local.settings.json.example
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ pyproject.toml           # Dependencies
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app/
‚îÇ  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ core/
‚îÇ  ‚îÇ  ‚îî‚îÄ logging.py
‚îÇ  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ functions/
‚îÇ  ‚îÇ  ‚îî‚îÄ http.py
‚îÇ  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ schemas/
‚îÇ  ‚îÇ  ‚îî‚îÄ request_models.py
‚îÇ  ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ services/
‚îÇ     ‚îî‚îÄ hello_service.py
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ tests/
   ‚îî‚îÄ test_http.py
```

## Project Requirements

- Project names must start with alphanumeric character
- Only letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores allowed
- Python 3.10+ required

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://yeongseon.github.io/azure-functions-scaffold/getting-started/)
- [CLI Reference](https://yeongseon.github.io/azure-functions-scaffold/cli/)
- [Templates](https://yeongseon.github.io/azure-functions-scaffold/templates/)
- [Architecture](https://yeongseon.github.io/azure-functions-scaffold/architecture/)
- [Deployment Guide](https://yeongseon.github.io/azure-functions-scaffold/deployment/)

## Ecosystem

Part of the Azure Functions Python DX Toolkit:
- azure-functions-validation ‚Äî Request/response validation
- azure-functions-openapi ‚Äî OpenAPI spec and Swagger UI
- azure-functions-doctor ‚Äî Pre-deploy diagnostics CLI
- azure-functions-langgraph ‚Äî Deploy LangGraph agents as Functions
